chapter_title.en: Chapter 16 – The Hoopoe and the Shrike

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (21/370)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (21/370)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَخِي مُوسَى ( عليه السلام ) عَنِ الْهُدْهُدِ وَ قَتْلِهِ وَ ذَبْحِهِ فَقَالَ لَا يُؤْذَى وَ لَا يُذْبَحُ فَنِعْمَ الطَّيْرُ هُوَ .

en.hubeali: And from him, from Yaqoub Bin Yazeed,

(It has been narrated) from Ali son of Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> who said, ‘I asked my brother<sup>asws</sup> Musa<sup>asws</sup> about the Hoopoe, and killing it and slaughtering it. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Neither harm nor slaughter, for the best of the birds, (it) is it’.
en.sarwar: 2. It is narrated from the narrator of the previous Hadith from Ya’qub ibn Yazid from Ali ibn Ja’far who has said the following: “I once asked my brother, abu al-Hassan, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about Hoopoe, killing and slaughtering it. He (the Imam) said, ‘It must not be troubled or slaughtered, it is a very good bird.’”
